title: Rethinking KL Regularization in RLHF: From Value Estimation to Gradient Optimization
abstract: This paper examines KL regularization in RLHF, shifting the focus from value estimation to gradient optimization. The authors perform a systematic, gradient-based analysis of the KL loss to address issues with current implementations.
main_contributions: A Gradient-Centric Framework for KL Regularization
A systematic, gradient-based analysis of KL loss
methodology: The paper performs a systematic, gradient-based analysis of KL regularization in RLHF, establishing a unified framework to connect different implementation styles.
key_findings: The choice of KL loss mathematical form should be guided by gradient-based optimization, not value estimation
The conventional 'k1 in reward' and 'k2 as loss' formulations are gradient equivalent and represent the principled approach
Popular alternatives like 'k3 as loss' are biased approximations
applications: RLHF in Large Language Models (LLMs) to align models with human values and improve performance in specialized reasoning tasks
focus: KL regularization in RLHF for Large Language Models
